aims: knowledge of their national histories from earliest times to 2000 with emphasis on the relationship between the peoples and states;
intellectual capacity and skills of historical interpretation and analysis;
ability to use acquired skills in relating the past to the present;
appreciation of factors that make for national unity and global understanding;
exposure and appreciation of the similarities and differences in the National, social and political institutions;
knowledge of the main historical developments in West Africa from earliest times to 2000;
ability to relate events in their country and West Africa to those of the outside world;
ability to present clear, relevant and logical arguments.

paper_1.description: This will be a multiple-choice objective test of fifty items. Candidates will be required to answer all the questions in 1 hour for 40 marks.
paper_1.coverage: West Africa and the Wider World from the earliest times to 2000.

paper_2.description: This will be a 2 hour essay type test containing sets of questions on the histories of member countries. Each set shall be made up of three sections, Sections A, B and C. Candidates will answer four questions in all, choosing at least one question from each section. The paper will carry 60 marks.

paper_1_west_africa_and_wider_world.title: Historiography and Historical Skills
paper_1_west_africa_and_wider_world.details: What is History and why do we study History? Sources of History; Historical skills (ancient and modern approaches); Prospect of ICT in historical studies.

paper_1_west_africa_and_wider_world.title: Trans-Saharan Trade
paper_1_west_africa_and_wider_world.details: Origin, organization and the effects on the development of West African states.

paper_1_west_africa_and_wider_world.title: European Contact with West Africa
paper_1_west_africa_and_wider_world.details: Reasons for their coming, immediate effects and West African reaction

paper_1_west_africa_and_wider_world.title: Christian Missionary Activities in West Africa
paper_1_west_africa_and_wider_world.details: The suppression of slave trade. Christian Missionary activities and their impact on West Africa.

paper_1_west_africa_and_wider_world.title: The Scramble for and Partition of West Africa
paper_1_west_africa_and_wider_world.details: The Industrial Revolution, Scramble for colonies, Colonial subjugation, Occupation and West African reaction.

paper_1_west_africa_and_wider_world.title: Colonial Rule in West Africa
paper_1_west_africa_and_wider_world.details: Patterns of colonial rule, consolidation of European culture in Africa, colonial economy and the underdevelopment of Africa: colonial Africa and the two World Wars.

paper_1_west_africa_and_wider_world.title: Problems of independent West African States
paper_1_west_africa_and_wider_world.details: Nature of politics: neo-colonialism and economic underdevelopment, unequal development within states and instability, the Military in West African politics, boundary disputes and threat to West African Unity.

paper_1_west_africa_and_wider_world.title: West Africa and international organizations
subtopics: (i) United Nations Organization (U.N.O.)/United Nations (U.N);
(ii) Organization of African Unity (O.A.U)/African Union (A.U.);
(iii) Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS);
Etc.

section_b.period: Ghana and the wider world: From earliest times to AD 1900

topics.title: Introduction to the History of Ghana
subtopics: Sources and Methods

Pre-history of Ghana -50,000B.C. - A.D 1700 (Hunters and Gatherers, Kintampo culture- farmers and village builders (2000 BC - AD 500), The first townsmen in Ghana: Begho, Bono-Manso etc. (AD1000-1700))

topics.title: The peopling of Ghana
subtopics: Peoples of Ghana: Northern zone, Forest zone, Coastal zone
The rise of states and kingdoms (General characteristics): Northern zone (Dagomba, Manprugu, Gonja, Nanumba), Forest zone (Denkyira, Akwamu, Akyem, Asante), Coastal zone (Fante, Ga, Anlo)

topics.title: Social, Cultural, Political and Economic Developments in Ghana in the Sixteenth Centuries
subtopics: Political systems: Centralized communities (Asante, Dagomba), Non-centralized communities (Sisala, Chamba), Theocratic communities (Ga-Adangbe, Guan), Comparison of the three systems.
Social organizations: religion, kinship systems (matriclans and patriclans), festivals, rites and ceremonies associated with various stages in the life cycle (marriage, birth, puberty and death)
History of medicine: botanical and zoological aspects of medicine
Pre-colonial technological advancement: brass casting, gold working, pottery etc. (processes, products, importance)
Art forms: Adinkra symbols, textiles, Kete, Adowa dance forms.
Economy: subsistence economy, exchange economy (long distance trading, local trading, importance of long distance trade)

topics.title: European contact
subtopics: Europeans on Ghana Coast: reasons for coming, immediate effects
Changing patterns of trade: AD1500-1900 (Atlantic slave trade- nature, volume, contributions to development of the Americas, effects on Ghana; trade with Europeans - gold, ivory etc.)
The Scramble for and partition of West Africa: causes, Berlin Conference, major recommendations, effects on West Africa.

topics.title: Social and Political Development AD 1500-1900
subtopics: Activities of the Christian missionaries: opening churches, schools, colleges, medical facilities, literacy work
Political Developments: Effects of European presence on local politics, The Bond of 1844, Aborigines Rights Protection Society.

topics.title: Social, Economic and Political Developments (AD 1900-1957)
subtopics: Nationalist activities and political changes from 1900 to 1957: Early Nationalist organizations (ARPS, NCBWA, Gold Coast Youth Conference, West African Youth League), Early Nationalists (John Mensah Sarbah, J. Casely Hayford, Kobina Sekyi), Later Nationalist Parties (UGCC, CPP, NLM), Later Nationalists (J.B. Danquah, Kwame Nkrumah, Paa Grant)
Social and Economic Developments: education, health and sanitation, religion, agriculture, transport and communications, mining, timber, the work of Sir Gordon Guggisberg

topics.title: Post-Independence Ghana
subtopics: The Nkrumah Era: Social developments, Economic developments, Political developments, contributions to African unity and world peace, development of one party state, the fall of Nkrumah regime

Post Nkrumah Era: causes of rapid changes of government, social and economic character of each regime (NLC, Second Republic 1969-1972, NRC & SMC 1972-1979, Uprising of 1979 & AFRC, Third Republic 1979-1981, PNDC 1981-1991)

topics.title: Ghana in the Comity of Nations
organizations: Organization of African Unity (OAU); African Union (AU)
Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS)
